# Project Opportunity Map — `CCDash`

> **Companion artifacts:** project-purpose-and-feature-catalog (feature inventory) · project-fundamentals-and-design-context (design DNA) · research-agent-context-pack (researcher briefing)

> **Purpose.** Forward-looking synthesis. Surfaces ideas, expansion paths, and strategic directions grounded in the catalog and fundamentals artifacts. Every opportunity must be evidence-linked and risk-tagged. Do not fabricate.

> **How to read.** Each opportunity carries: rationale (what makes it ripe), evidence link (where in the code/docs the signal lives), strategic value, risk class (`low`/`medium`/`high`), and architectural impact (`none`/`local`/`global`).

## Near-Adjacent Opportunities

Ideas that extend existing functionality without changing the architecture. Cheapest to pursue. High likelihood of fitting the project's character.

| Opportunity | Rationale | Evidence | Strategic value | Risk | Arch impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Finish the Typer CLI surface end-to-end | ADR-005 accepted, Phase 1 foundation commit landed, unblocks headless workflows | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-005-cli-framework-selection-typer.md`, `docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/features/ccdash-cli-mcp-enablement-v1` | high — unlocks IDE/MCP/agent surfaces | low | local |
| Ship the dual-transport MCP server | ADRs 002/004 set direction; `session_intelligence_queries` + `stack_recommendations` are ready to expose | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-002-mcp-server-separate-from-extension.md`, `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-004-mcp-server-dual-transport-strategy.md`, `backend/services/session_intelligence_queries.py`, `backend/services/stack_recommendations.py` | high — turns CCDash into an agent-queryable knowledge base | medium | local |
| Promote one experimental flag per release | Three flags currently default off; graduating them is a compounding UX win | `backend/config.py` (`CCDASH_TEST_VISUALIZER_ENABLED`, `CCDASH_INTEGRITY_SIGNALS_ENABLED`, `CCDASH_LIVE_TEST_UPDATES_ENABLED`) | medium | low | none |
| Add per-entity sync health badges in Ops | The sync engine already tracks hash/mtime state; surfacing per-entity-type freshness is additive | `backend/db/sync_engine.py`, `components/OpsPanel` | medium — operator confidence | low | none |
| Workflow "cost regression" alert | Workflow effectiveness already ranks by efficiency; threshold-breach notifications are a small extension | `backend/services/workflow_effectiveness.py`, `backend/routers/analytics.py` notifications | medium | low | none |
| Session replay filters by linked feature/document | Entity links already exist; the inspector already filters | `backend/db/repositories/entity_graph.py`, `components/SessionInspector` | medium | low | none |
| Export a "project health briefing" markdown from Dashboard | Gemini insights already produce executive summaries; writing them to `docs/` is trivially additive | `services/geminiService.ts`, `components/Dashboard` | low–medium | low | none |

## Bolder Novel Ideas

Ideas that combine existing capabilities in new ways or address adjacent problems the project doesn't yet serve. Not architectural rewrites — additive but ambitious.

| Opportunity | Rationale | Evidence | Strategic value | Risk | Arch impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| "Self-aware" agent mode | Expose session intelligence over MCP so an in-flight agent can query its own prior churn, cost, and scope drift and self-correct | `backend/services/session_churn_facts.py`, `backend/services/session_scope_drift.py`, `backend/services/stack_recommendations.py`, `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-003-transport-neutral-agent-query-layer.md` | high — differentiates CCDash against plain observability | medium | local |
| Workflow recommender as pre-execution gate | Use `backend/services/stack_recommendations.py` output to annotate the pre-run review modal with "historically this workflow had X% failure rate" | `backend/services/stack_recommendations.py`, `backend/services/execution_policy.py`, `components/FeatureExecutionWorkbench` | high | low | none |
| Cross-project intelligence view | The project manager already handles multiple projects; a meta-dashboard would pool effectiveness across projects | `backend/project_manager.py`, `backend/services/workflow_effectiveness.py` | medium–high | medium | local |
| Hook-aware subagent topology edits | Session parser already extracts subagent topology; let operators edit/annotate it to train better recommendations | `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`, `components/SessionInspector` | medium | medium | local |
| Plan-document drift detector | Compare plan/progress files against linked sessions' file footprint and flag "plan says phase 3 done but no session touched X" | `backend/parsers/progress.py`, `backend/document_linking.py` | high — keeps plan-driven workflow honest | medium | local |
| Ship a VSCode extension using SSE + MCP | ADR-001 exists; once MCP ships, extension is thin glue over it | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-001-vscode-extension-realtime-sse-over-polling.md`, `backend/routers/live.py` | high — in-editor forensics | medium | local |
| Session cost budget enforcement | Attribution + pricing catalog already exist; add per-project budgets and pre-execution guardrails | `backend/services/session_usage_attribution.py`, `backend/services/pricing_catalog.py`, `backend/services/execution_policy.py` | medium–high | low | none |

## Ideas That Require Architectural Change

Opportunities that cannot be pursued without altering load-bearing parts of the system. Most expensive; require the highest justification.

| Opportunity | Required change | Blocked by | Migration feasibility | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Multi-tenant SaaS deployment | Real auth, RLS/tenant isolation, non-filesystem source of truth | `backend/db/repositories/identity_access.py` is a stub; filesystem-as-truth assumption; single-user operational assumptions | feasible but large — PRD exists (`docs/project_plans/PRDs/enhancements/shared-auth-rbac-sso-v1.md`); would likely live on the `api`/`worker` profiles plus new `shared_postgres` work | high |
| Remote execution via platform connectors | Execution runtime today is local subprocess sandboxing; remote requires a connector abstraction, credential management, and streaming back through `backend/routers/live.py` | `backend/services/execution_runtime.py`, local-first product principle, approval-gate model | feasible — PRD Phase 3 exists but no code; scope is substantial | high |
| Pluggable agent SDK orchestration | Would let CCDash drive Claude Code / Codex runs rather than only observe them | `docs/project_plans/PRDs/enhancements/feature-execution-workbench-phase-4-sdk-orchestration-v1.md` is PRD-only; trust/approval model needs extension | feasible if approval-gates are preserved | high |
| Swap sync engine for an event-sourced pipeline | Current sync is batch-style incremental; event sourcing would give perfect replay but requires reshaping `backend/db/sync_engine.py` and all repos | Every entity relies on the current sync contract; this is the highest-footprint file in the repo | hard — likely a months-long refactor; high regression risk | high |
| Replace split-shell contexts with a state library (zustand/redux/tanstack-query) | The `DataContext` facade would have to be retired; every `useData()` call site updated | Many components call `useData()`; facade was presumably introduced precisely to avoid this | medium — could be done incrementally behind the facade | medium |
| Expose CCDash data as a public API | Would require auth, rate limiting, documentation, and a stable contract — none of which exist | No auth today; router-level contracts not versioned | low-feasibility today | high |

## Business Model Extensions

Directions that change how the project creates or captures value. May or may not require code changes — many are purely positioning shifts.

| Direction | What it changes | Required capabilities | Existing assets that help | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CCDash for teams (hosted) | Shifts from local-first-only to "local-first with team view" | Multi-tenant auth, shared Postgres, reliable sync | `enterprise` storage profile, shared-Postgres toggle, `api`/`worker` profiles | high |
| Workflow effectiveness as a published dataset | Lets operators or vendors consume cross-project workflow stats | Anonymization, export format, consent model | `backend/services/workflow_effectiveness.py`, SAM telemetry exporter pattern | medium |
| "CCDash Intelligence" library for agents | Publish the session-intelligence computation layer as a standalone package | Packaging, stable interface | `backend/services/session_intelligence_queries.py`, `backend/services/session_transcript_projection.py` | medium |
| Integration with vendor SDKs (Anthropic, OpenAI) for direct session pulls | Removes the filesystem intermediary for agents running under managed SDKs | Credential management, non-filesystem parsers | existing parser-routing layer | medium |
| Premium local-first product (pay for Ops, export, backfill) | Tiered product with local-first free core | Licensing, feature-flag gating | existing `CCDASH_*` feature flag vocabulary | medium |

## Workflow Automation Opportunities

Internal opportunities: where repeated manual work in the project's own development or operation could be automated. Often low-risk, high-leverage.

| Opportunity | Current pain | Evidence | Effort | Value |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Auto-sync CHANGELOG.md entries from plan completion | `CHANGELOG.md` is manually updated with each rollout; plan/progress files already have completion state | `CHANGELOG.md`, `.claude/progress/` | low | high |
| Auto-generate ADR scaffolds from architectural decisions flagged in plans | Plans frequently reference "decision" but ADRs are separate | `docs/project_plans/adrs/`, `docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/` | low | medium |
| Auto-link test failures to feature/session pairs | `test_integrity` clustering exists; wiring it into the Project Board would close the loop | `backend/services/integrity_detector.py`, `backend/db/repositories/test_integrity.py` | medium | high |
| Auto-promote experimental flags after N passing days | Three flags default off; promotion is currently manual | `backend/config.py` | medium | medium |
| Auto-regenerate `docs/schemas/` from Pydantic models | `backend/models.py` is large and central; schemas are versioned separately | `backend/models.py`, `docs/schemas/` | medium | medium |
| CI surface for the Storage Capability Matrix | Matrix exists at `/api/health`; exporting as a badge/status endpoint helps operators | `backend/runtime/storage_contract.py`, `backend/routers/api.py` | low | medium |

## Research Questions Ranked by Strategic Value

| Rank | Question | What answering it unlocks | Cost to answer |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Can the sync engine be factored into per-entity-type sync modules without regressing hash tracking? | The single biggest tech-debt win; de-risks every future change | 1–2 days of tracing + a spike |
| 2 | What is the minimal MCP resource shape that lets an agent self-assess prior sessions? | Differentiates CCDash from plain observability; enables "self-aware" agent mode | 2–3 days including MCP SDK work |
| 3 | How close is the `enterprise` profile to running a multi-user team view today? | Scopes the SaaS / team play | 1 day of test matrix + auth stub inspection |
| 4 | Is the canonical transcript contract stable enough to freeze and version? | Unblocks third-party consumers and the agent-query layer | 1 day |
| 5 | What breaks if `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py` misses a new Claude Code log field? | Governs compatibility-surface resilience | Half a day of fixture work |
| 6 | Can workflow effectiveness produce per-workflow budget recommendations? | Turns analytics into actionable guardrails | 1 day of data exploration |
| 7 | Where does CI run today and what is gated? | Enables confident opportunity-map execution | 15 minutes of operator answer |

## Risks of Pursuing Each Direction

| Direction | Failure mode | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| MCP/agent-query rollout | Schema churn across Phase 1 foundation → downstream consumers | medium | Version the agent-query contracts; freeze before broad consumption |
| Sync engine refactor | Subtle hash / ordering regression silently breaks intelligence | high | Per-entity golden fixtures; keep old engine behind flag until parity proven |
| Multi-tenant SaaS | Auth/tenant isolation gaps leak session data | high | Stage behind `enterprise` profile with explicit opt-in, pen-test before exposure |
| Remote execution connectors | Policy-gate bypass enables unsafe remote runs | high | Keep approval gate authoritative regardless of connector |
| Plan-drift detector | False positives erode trust in plan-driven workflow | medium | Make the detector surface suggestions, not blockers |
| Workflow budget enforcement | Budget gating blocks legitimate long runs | medium | Make budgets soft by default, per-project override |
| VSCode extension | Feature requires an external surface this repo cannot fully test | medium | Keep extension thin; push logic into MCP |
| Mega-file refactor (analytics router / models) | Frontend breakage because of DTO drift | medium | Contract tests; incremental extraction; `CHANGELOG.md` discipline |

## Confidence Notes

This opportunity map is **inference-heavy by design** — it extrapolates from the highest-confidence claims in the catalog and fundamentals artifacts. The "Near-Adjacent" rows are the most evidence-grounded: every one points at existing code and active plans. The "Bolder Novel Ideas" section leans on inference from existing capability combinations — the patterns are visible in the code but no one has said "we'll do this." The "Architectural Change" section is most speculative and should be treated as scoping input, not commitments; each row names the load-bearing constraint that would have to change. The "Business Model Extensions" section is the most inference-heavy of all, since nothing in the repo speaks to business model directly; these are read off the technical shape of the product, not from any stated strategy. Researcher should re-verify `[evidence H]` tags in the catalog and fundamentals artifacts before pursuing any item here as a plan.

## Open Questions

1. Which of the Near-Adjacent opportunities is already tracked in a plan under `docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/enhancements/` that this pass did not enumerate?
2. What is the team's position on multi-tenant / team / SaaS — is it explicitly out of scope, or on the roadmap but not yet prioritized?
3. Does the `feat/cli-mcp-enablement-codex` branch plan already include a session-intelligence MCP resource shape?
4. Are there cost or latency targets that would constrain the sync-engine refactor (e.g., must stay <N ms for a 1000-file project)?
5. Is there any appetite to expose anonymous workflow-effectiveness statistics upstream, or is that a principled no?
